actual_current_surface_status: exact negative boundary / FH-LSZ uniform-gap self-certification no-go
exact_next_action: Attack the microscopic scalar denominator theorem directly, or continue seed-controlled production chunks toward a postprocess that supplies an independent threshold/gap certificate.
proposal_allowed_reason: Finite shell rows do not certify the uniform gap or pole-saturation premise.
strict_non_claims: does not infer a continuum threshold from finite shell rows
does not use observed top mass or observed y_t
does not use H_unit, Ward authority, alpha_LM, plaquette, or u0
verdict: Finite same-source Euclidean shell rows cannot self-certify the uniform continuum gap needed by the pole-saturation gate.  A dataset generated from a deliberately gapped positive Stieltjes model is exactly reproducible by a near-pole positive continuum model, and the allowed pole-residue interval then has zero lower bound.  Therefore the uniform gap must come from an independent scalar-denominator theorem, continuum-threshold certificate, or production acceptance analysis; it cannot be inferred from the finite shell rows alone.
